Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Technically I think I am ok with this patch, though I wonder if we really needed a resend callback instead of just queuing a new request upon noticing an error or timeout (in handle_ast_error function). Seems to be more consistent with other lock handling and gather all such logic in just one place as well, also less race prone should the timout hit earlier than the resend.

Patch Set 1:

> Seems to be more consistent with other lock handling

not sure where it will be more consistent, lock cancel was the only place which had some timeout logic in the caller, but has been sent asynchronously for a while;

> and gather all such logic in just one place as well

which one place? so that each caller take care about it by itself - it is multiple places.

> also less race prone should the timout hit earlier than the resend.

resend happens on each ptlrpc timeout, handled in 1 place - ptlrpc; the caller gets such timeout only if rq_nr_resend (re-connects + re-sends) timeouts occur. we need to update data on each ptlrpc re-send.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

(11 comments)

Firstly, thank you for this patch.  Server-side resend is something that I've wanted to have for a long time.  I think this will make Lustre much more reliable in the face of network problems, especially on routed networks.

Unfortunately, it appears that the patch has failed recover-small.sh test_10a twice with "no eviction", once on patch #4 and once on patch #5:

 https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/26bcec3c-0946-11e4-939d-5254006e85c2
 https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/6e8d5744-0749-11e4-92f3-5254006e85c2

This may just be a problem with the test itself, that the client is not being evicted because this patch is removing  OBD_FAIL_LDLM_BL_CALLBACK_NET and therefore the call to drop_ldlm_cancel() is not doing anything.

parents.subject: LU-5496 ldlm: reconstruct proper flags on enqueue resend

commit.subject: LU-5520 ldlm: resend AST callbacks
commit.message:
LU-5520 ldlm: resend AST callbacks

While clients will resend client->server RPCs, servers would not
resend server->client RPCs such as LDLM callbacks (blocking
or completion callbacks/ASTs).  This could result in clients being
evicted from the server if blocking callbacks were dropped by the
network (a failed router or lossy network) and the client did not
cancel the requested lock in time.
In order to fix this problem, this patch adds the ability to resend
LDLM callbacks from the server and give the client a chance to
respond within the timeout period before it is evicted:

- resend BL AST within lock callback timeout period;
- still do not resend CANCEL_ON_BLOCK;
- regular resend for CP AST without BL AST embedded;
- prolong lock callback timeout on resend;

some fixes:
- recovery-small test_10 to actually evict the client
  with dropped BL AST;
- ETIMEDOUT to be returned if send limit is expired;

Signed-off-by: Vitaly Fertman <vitaly_fertman@xyratex.com>
Change-Id: Ie65fac94ea68defffd1769cbbb0f74381c11262c
Tested-by: Elena Gryaznova <Elena_Gryaznova@xyratex.com>
Reviewed-by: Alexey Lyashkov <Alexey_Lyashkov@xyratex.com>
Reviewed-by: Andriy Skulysh <Andriy_Skulysh@xyratex.com>
Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-417
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/9335
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Johann Lombardi <johann.lombardi@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
